title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Celtics plan to work out UConn forward Alex Karaban ahead of the 2026 NBA Draft next week.
Karaban is ranked No. 32 in ESPN's top 100 draft prospects; Celtics hold No. 27 and No. 40 draft picks.
Karaban played 151 games at UConn, averaging 12.5 points and 5.0 rebounds, winning two NCAA championships.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth performed at MGM Music Hall in Fenway, blending jazz and pop for an energetic and nostalgic audience experience.
The concert mixed songs from Puth's new album "Whatever’s Clever!" with older hits, showcasing his deep musical understanding.
Opening acts Ally Salort and Daniel Seavey impressed with indie-pop sounds and live looping, setting the tone for Puth's performance.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers performed their entire 'Bringing Down the Horse' album at The Cabot in Beverly on May 22, 2026.
After performing their album, The Wallflowers played Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark' in full, highlighting tracks like 'You Got Lucky'.
Jakob Dylan's current tour marks the 30th anniversary of 'Bringing Down the Horse', integrating Petty's lesser-known songs into the setlist.

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston lost 1,338 residents in 2025, with a total population decline of 5,644 since 2020.
Everett gained the most residents in Massachusetts, increasing by 2,362, followed by Worcester with 1,916.
High housing costs and reduced immigration are key factors in population decline in pricier cities like Boston.

title: These were the Mass. companies on the new Forbes list of largest family businesses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Fidelity Investments and Market Basket's parent company ranked on Forbes' list of largest U.S. family businesses.
Fidelity, led by the Johnson family, ranked 14th with $37.7 billion annual revenue; Demoulas Super Markets ranked 69th.
Forbes' list focuses on top multigenerational family businesses, including private and public companies with family ownership.

title: UMass Amherst launches major housing revitalization project
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: UMass Amherst partners with American Campus Communities for a long-term campus housing revitalization project.
The project plans to modernize residences while keeping them affordable, enhancing amenities for students.
Redevelopment will occur in phases, allowing students to stay on campus during renovations.

title: Review & Setlist: Lucinda Williams brings protest songs, fighting spirit to Medford
author: Terence Cawley
bullets: Lucinda Williams performed at Chevalier Theatre in Medford, focusing on protest songs from her new album 'World's Gone Wrong'.
Unable to play guitar due to a stroke, Williams delivered protest themes through her music and speeches against political figures.
The setlist included covers of Bob Marley and Neil Young, ending with 'Rockin' in the Free World' to an enthusiastic audience.

title: Twins unload on Justin Slaten as Red Sox lose 8-6 at Fenway Park
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Justin Slaten gave up four runs in a single inning, leading to an 8-6 loss to the Twins.
Manager Chad Tracy pulled Payton Tolle after the sixth inning despite his nine strikeouts and solid performance.
Boston's offensive efforts were overshadowed by Minnesota; Red Sox stranded two runners in the eighth inning.

title: Roger Clemens throws out first pitch at Fenway Park to son, Twins player Kody Clemens
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roger Clemens threw the first pitch at Fenway Park ahead of the Red Sox game against the Twins.
Clemens pitched to his son, Kody Clemens, a Twins player, wearing a No. 21 'Rocket Man' jersey.
The event included a Roger Clemens bobblehead giveaway and Clemens signing autographs for fans.

title: Celtics’ Derrick White named to 2025-26 NBA All-Defensive First Team
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Derrick White named to 2025-26 NBA All-Defensive First Team for the first time in his career.
White recorded a career-high 98 blocks and 88 steals, leading all guards in defensive stats.
Celtics' defense improved by 7.4 points per 100 possessions with White on the floor.
